definition: 2019-2020 vintage CLOs exiting their reinvestment periods coincide with the 2026-2028 commodity-sector maturity wall. Post-reinvestment CLOs are trapped in commodity credits while active CLOs create forced-selling cascades, amplifying credit stress beyond what physical fundamentals would produce.
evidence: 2019-2020 vintage CLOs exiting reinvestment as maturity wall hits
Post-reinvestment CLOs trapped; active CLOs create forced selling
2015-2016 precedent: 30-40% of secondary market volume from forced selling
open_questions: What share of commodity-sector leveraged loans is held in CLO structures?
Can CLO managers mitigate through early portfolio rotation?
